{"step_by_step_thinking": "Direct ophthalmoscopy uses a handheld device that provides a direct view of the fundus. The image is visual and erect, which makes option A true. The field of vision for direct ophthalmoscopy is approximately 2 disk diameters, making option B true. However, the magnification provided by direct ophthalmoscopy is typically around 5x, so option C is also true. Direct ophthalmoscopy does not use an internal light source; instead, it relies on an external light source, making option D false.", "answer_choice": "D"}
